channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Democrats call for special counsel probe into Kushner over Saudi ties
item.description: Senior Democrats in the House and Senate are calling on the Justice Department to investigate former President Trump’s son-in-law and senior adviser in the White House, Jared Kushner, over possible violations related to lobbying for a foreign government. Sen. Ron Wyden (D-Ore.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Massive fire breaks out at US Army warehouse in South Korea
item.description: A fire broke out at a renovated U.S. Army storage center in Busan, South Korea, Thursday night, U.S. Army Garrison Daegu confirmed to Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada will reduce immigration targets as Trudeau acknowledges his policy failed
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Thursday that Canada will significantly reduce the number of new immigrants it allows into the country after acknowledging that his government failed to get the balance right coming out of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentine police raid the Buenos Aires hotel where One Direction’s Liam Payne died
item.description: The police have raided the Buenos Aires hotel where ex-One Direction singer Liam Payne stayed before dying last week after falling from a third-floor balcony.
item.dc:creator: Almudena Calatrava, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey struck suspected Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day on Thursday following an attack on the premises of a key defense company that killed at least five people, the state-run news agency reported.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive displacement from Israel-Hezbollah war transforms Beirut’s famed commercial street
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Inside what was once one of Beirut’s oldest and best-known cinemas, dozens of Lebanese, Palestinians and Syrians displaced by the Israel-Hezbollah war spend their time following the news on their phones, cooking, chatting and walking around to pass the time.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey hits US-allied Kurds in Syria, Iraq following terrorist attack on defense group
item.description: Turkey continued its strikes in Syria and Iraq for a second day following the terrorist attack on a defense company in Ankara, in what Turkey said were targeted hits on PKK terrorist locations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada's Trudeau vows lead his Liberal Party into the next election
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Thursday that he will lead his Liberal Party into the next election, dismissing a request by some party members to not run for a fourth term.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trash carried by a North Korean balloon again falls on the presidential compound in Seoul
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Trash carried by a North Korean balloon fell on the presidential compound in central Seoul on Thursday for the second time, raising concerns about the vulnerability of key South Korean sites.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Britain's leaders likely to face slavery reparations questions at a summit of former colonies
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Britain's leaders will likely face uncomfortable questions about reparations for the trans-Atlantic slave trade at a summit of nations it once colonized, after Caribbean leaders said they would thrust the matter into the spotlight at the event in Samoa.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Paris conference for Lebanon raises $1 billion in pledges for humanitarian and military support
item.description: PARIS (AP) — An international conference for Lebanon in Paris on Thursday raised $1 billion in pledges for humanitarian aid and military support to help the country where war between Hezbollah militants and Israel has displaced a million people, killed over 2,500, and deepened an economic crisis, Fr
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mozambique's ruling party candidate declared winner of presidential election as rigging claims swirl
item.description: Moazmbique’s ruling party candidate Daniel Chapo has been declared the winner of the country’s presidential election amid claims of rigging by the opposition.
item.dc:creator: Charles Mangwiro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venice extends its day-tripper tax through next year to combat overtourism
item.description: ROME (AP) — Venice will extend its day-tripper tax through next year, increasing the number of days on which tourists have to pay to enter the city and doubling the fee to 10 euros ($10.80) for last-minute visitors, city officials said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Colombia, Amazon River's extreme drought falls hard on Indigenous communities
item.description: LETICIA, Colombia (AP) — Marciano Flores stood knee-deep in the Amazon River with the rest of his crew, ready to haul in a giant net as a man in a canoe circled in an attempt to drive fish their way.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president raises possibility of supplying Ukraine with arms
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s president on Thursday raised the possibility of supplying Ukraine with weapons while stressing that his government “won’t sit idle” as North Korea allegedly sends troops to support Russia’s aggression toward its neighbor.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia says its coast guard drove away Chinese ship that interrupted survey in disputed sea
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian patrol ships drove a Chinese coast guard vessel away from a survey vessel in a disputed area of the South China Sea for the second time in three days, Indonesian authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian lawmakers ratify pact with North Korea as US confirms that Pyongyang sent troops to Russia
item.description: Russian lawmakers have ratified a pact with North Korea envisioning mutual military assistance, a move that comes even as the U.S. confirmed the deployment of 3,000 North Korean troops to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Climate-threatened Pacific Islands criticize Australia at Commonwealth meeting
item.description: APIA, Samoa (AP) — Several Pacific island nations singled out Australia to do more to phase out fossil fuel exports during the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting which opened in Samoa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel accuses 6 Al Jazeera journalists in Gaza of being Palestinian militants
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The Israeli army on Wednesday accused six Al Jazeera journalists covering the war in Gaza of also being current or former paid fighters for Palestinian militant groups. Al Jazeera rejected the claims.
item.dc:creator: JACK JEFFERY and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Iraq and Syria following deadly attack on defense company
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey’s air force struck Kurdish militant targets in Iraq and Syria on Wednesday in an apparent retaliation for an attack at a key state-run defense company that killed five people and wounded more than 20 others.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Some Liberal lawmakers ask Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau not to run for a fourth term
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Some lawmakers in Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's own Liberal Party asked the leader not to run for a fourth term Wednesday, handing him one of the biggest tests of his political career.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Kristy strengthens into a Category 4 storm in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Hurricane Kristy strengthened into a Category 4 storm in the Pacific Ocean on Wednesday and was expected to remain away from land as it churns in the Pacific Ocean, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican troops kill 19 suspected cartel members, suffer no casualties: officials
item.description: Mexican army troops have reportedly killed 19 suspected cartel affiliates during a Monday shootout near Culiacan, the state capital of Sinaloa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli weapon seen in rare AP photos of Beirut airstrike appears to be a powerful smart bomb
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — In all but the blink of an eye, an Associated Press photographer's camera captured the moments that a battleship-gray Israeli bomb plummeted toward a Beirut building before detonating to bring the tower down.
item.dc:creator: ADAM SCHRECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Situation in Haiti worsens amid ‘acute violence’ as UN support mission falters under gang violence
item.description: The gang violence in Haiti has gotten worse despite the deployment of U.N.-sanctioned international police officers, forcing mass displacement, hunger among millions and even famine for thousands of Haitians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 US service members injured during counter-ISIS mission in Iraq
item.description: Two U.S. troops were injured Tuesday in a joint raid with Iraqi security forces that killed at least seven senior Islamic State group leaders, according to the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Group of metal detectorists uncover hoard of coins in England
item.description: A group of metal detectorists discovered a hoard of 21 coins near Okehampton Castle nearly a year ago, but the news of their finding was just recently unveiled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon chief: North Korean troops are in Russia
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin said North Korean troops appear to be in Russia. “We are seeing evidence that there are North Korean troops that have, that have gone to … Russia,” Austin told reporters in Rome on Wednesday. “What exactly they’re doing, left to be seen.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: Police said they haven't yet confirmed the cause and manner of death of the woman, who worked at the store, nor has her name been released.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, NewsNation

item.title: Woman becomes stuck upside down between boulders for 7 hours while trying to retrieve dropped phone
item.description: A woman who became trapped in between two boulders in Australia after falling into a crevice while trying to retrieve her phone has been saved, NSW Ambulance says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine blasts UN chief over potential meeting with Putin at BRICS, says stayed away from Kyiv's peace summit
item.description: Ukraine blasted U.N. Secretary-General Guterres for allegedly agreeing to meet with Russian President Putin during the BRICS summit as leaders from the West's top adversaries like China and Iran convene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Binational delegation heads to Mexico City to talk sewage, border waits, stalled port of entry
item.description: The delegation is led by the San Diego Regional Chamber of Commerce, which says their mission is to "champion U.S.-Mexico relations and advance issues critical to the success of the binational business community as the newly sworn-in Mexico Administration begins its session.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken arrives in Israel as Biden-Harris admin faces scrutiny over compromising Jerusalem’s security
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken arrived for talks with Israel on his 11th trip there since October 2023. The Jewish state is fighting a seven-front war against Iran-backed proxies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah naval base in Beirut as Iran rallies Gulf Arab nations
item.description: Hezbollah launched missiles on central Israel Tuesday. Meanwhile, Israel Defense Forces said it struck a Hezbollah naval base in Beirut, along with other terrorist targets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tropical Storm Oscar dumps heavy rain on Cuba as it heads toward the Bahamas
item.description: Its maximum sustained winds blew at 40 mph on Monday as Oscar moved west-northwest at 3 mph. The storm was located about 15 miles (25 kilometers) west-northwest of Guantánamo, Cuba, the U.S. National Hurricane Center in Miami said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japan’s ruling party HQ attacked with firebombs; man arrested: Reports
item.description: A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s ruling party in Tokyo Saturday, media outlets report.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama/The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation
